question: The minimum standards for remuneration schemes at financial institutions are not applied with equal intensity to every firm. Which principle determines the extent of their application in an individual case?

A bank belongs to a financial conglomerate that is itself an addressee of the Swiss remuneration minimum standards; the bank is subject to the conglomerate's remuneration regulations and the guidance of its remuneration committee, and the conglomerate's remuneration report adequately includes the bank. Must the bank still prepare its own remuneration report?

Mandatory foreign regulations in one of its labour markets conflict with the remuneration requirements a Swiss-supervised firm must apply to its consolidated foreign branches. After the firm informs FINMA, may FINMA exempt it partly or fully from applying those requirements in that labour market?
What role should the remuneration principles play for financial institutions that are not obliged to implement them?

May FINMA require a firm that is not otherwise obliged to implement the remuneration minimum standards to apply some or all of their provisions, for example because of its risk profile or business relationships?
In recognition of work performed, a firm waives repayment of a loan it had granted to an employee. Does the waived amount count towards the employee's total remuneration?
When determining the total pool of variable remuneration for a financial year, must sign-on and severance payments granted during that year be attributed to it?
A board of directors issues remuneration rules covering only senior management and material risk takers. Does this satisfy the requirements on the personal scope of such rules?

Rather than drafting its own rules, the board of a supervised subsidiary wants to adopt the parent group's existing group-wide remuneration scheme, which conforms to the Swiss remuneration minimum standards. Is this generally permissible?
In the case of a listed company, which body decides on establishing the remuneration committee?
By design, which four characteristics must a financial institution's remuneration scheme combine under the Swiss minimum standards?
An employee plans private hedging transactions that would offset the intended effect of his deferred share-based remuneration. Is this compatible with the remuneration minimum standards?
Some of a firm's existing employment agreements conflict with its newly issued remuneration rules. May the firm simply leave those agreements as they are?
Besides human resources experts, which functions should be involved in designing and applying a remuneration scheme so that it is implemented consistently across all business lines?

When a division head's remuneration is aligned with risk, must risks arising in the organizational units under her responsibility be taken into account even though she did not enter into them personally?
Which units should undertake and monitor the risk assessments used for risk-adjusting remuneration?
A bonus plan's allocation criteria reward conduct that breaches the firm's internal rules, although no external law is violated. Is such a design permissible?
Under which circumstances are risks considered inappropriate for the purposes of remuneration design at financial institutions?
gold: inconsistent with objectives and risk capacity
cannot be properly managed or controlled
accept: not consistent with strategic or operational objectives
incompatible with the firm's risk capacity
not manageable with the existing organization
cannot be controlled with existing procedures and employees
may unfairly disadvantage the firm's stakeholders

question: In its capital and liquidity planning, may a bank allow variable remuneration to endanger the achievement of its capital targets?
Must the costs of equity capital be included among the capital costs considered when determining the size of the total pool?
A firm posts poor results for the financial year. May it nevertheless keep its total pool of variable remuneration at the prior-year level?
The models and processes a firm uses to determine variable remuneration must accord with which two elements of the firm's overall framework?
Does the obligation to defer part of remuneration apply to every firm to the same extent, regardless of its risk profile?
An employee may freely dispose of part of her remuneration only after a waiting period, during which its value can still rise or fall. What is this form of remuneration called?
A junior employee earns a modest total remuneration and has no significant influence on the firm's risk profile. May the firm pay her without any deferral?

Where appropriate and conducive to risk awareness, should a firm's remuneration rules make it possible to cancel, in whole or in part, the deferred remuneration of a person in whose area of responsibility losses were generated?
A firm records losses in its annual financial reporting. Must the allocation of variable remuneration that is not subject to deferral be reduced to a minimum?
Do actuarial staff belong to the control functions whose remuneration must be designed to avoid conflicts of interest?
Which corporate body must prepare the remuneration report as part of a firm's annual reporting?

With regard to severance payments made during the financial year to senior management and persons with significant influence on the risk profile, which two figures must the remuneration report disclose?
Regardless of how the annual report is published, to whom must a firm's remuneration report be disclosed in every case?
In a justified exceptional case, a firm deviates from one of the remuneration principles. Is it sufficient to document the deviation internally without disclosing it?
Can FINMA, in justified cases, impose requirements on an individual firm's remuneration scheme that go beyond the published minimum standards?
Which supervisory measure is explicitly named as available against firms that fail to observe the remuneration provisions?
The remuneration disclosure requirements applied for the first time to the financial reporting of which financial year?
